veal-with-cornmeal-mash-argiro.name: Veal With Cornmeal Mash

ingredients.item: 1.5 kg of olive oil or sprat comb cut into bites
A little flour for the breading
1.5 kg of olive oil or sprat comb cut into bites
A little flour for the breading

instructions: Sprinkle the meat with some flour and shake the pieces to remove the unnecessary.
Heat the 4 tablespoons of olive oil in a pot and add a few pieces of meat for a few minutes. Everything that's pink, we put on a platter and keep it warm.
In the same pot, add the remaining olive oil (2 tabletspoons) and sauté the onions, carrots, cellery, garlic, hot pepper, bay leaves, rosemary and sage.
Set them on medium to low heat for 6-7 minutes, stirring regularly so that they soften without color.
Once the vegetables soften, put the meat back in the pot, along with the liquids left on the platter, and turn the heat on.
After 3-4 minutes, put the wine out and leave a few minutes on high heat until it rejuvenates. Add the tomatoes and meat broth.
Cover the pot, lower the heat and simmer the meat for about 2 hours until it softens and becomes a sausage.
Towards the end of the boil, salt and pepper.
Once ready, withdraw from the fire, sprinkle with parsley, add the orange peel and mix to release its essential oils.
Cover the pot (excluding heat) and leave it for 10 minutes, so that the aromas can be combined.
Serve with cornmeat mashed potatoes.

veal-with-mustard-sauce-pomontoria-argir.name: Veal With Mustard Sauce & Pomontoria

ingredients.item: 1 bottle (750 ml) white dry wine (such as mucofilero or chardonnay)
Two or three legumes of parsley
1 twig fresh thyme or a little dry
1 bottle (750 ml) white dry wine (such as mucofilero or chardonnay)
Two or three legumes of parsley
1 twig fresh thyme or a little dry

instructions: To make the beef with mustard sauce, we ask the butcher to cut the meat into big bites.
In a pot and in medium to strong heat sauté the meat into the olive oil a little bit bit at a time (so that the temperature of the oil does not fall) until golden on all sides.
We take it out with a clamp or a drill spoon on a platter and keep it covered until we brown it all.
Place the opinions in the pot and sauce them for 5 minutes until golden and clear.
Add the wine and spoon or spatula to the base of the pot until the caramelized and solidified juices of the sotera are dissolved.
Let the wine come to a boiling point, lower the heat and simmer around 8'-10' until the juice is halved.
Add the mustard and mix with wire to dissolve.
Add the garlic and the pomontories.
Wrap the bouquet of herbs in a tulip. Tie it with string and pour it into the pot, fastening it to the handle (so that we can easily remove it at the end of cooking).
Add the sauteed meat with all the juices it has excelled on the platter and add salt and pepper.
Cover the pot and simmer for 2 hours until the meat softens well.
Remove the aromatic bouquet and transfer the meat to a platter and cover.
Continue boiling the sauce until it thickens and reduces its volume by 1/3, about 10 minutes.
Add the meat back to the dressing that has been tied and mix lightly.
Serve the beef with mustard sauce with rice.

vegan-lobster-rolls.name: Vegan 'lobster' Rolls

ingredients.item: Salt and ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Preheat the oven to 204°C (200 degrees C).
Pat hearts of palm dry and place in a medium bowl. Toss with avocado oil and coat with seafood seasoning; season with salt and pepper. Transfer to a baking pan.
Roast in the preheated oven for 20 minutes.

Stir together vegan mayonnaise, celery, shallot, lemon zest, juice of 1/4 lemon, capers, dill, and sesame seeds in a large bowl until well combined; season with salt. Add roasted hearts of palm and toss to coat.
Brush the tops of sandwich rolls with olive oil. Toast in a large skillet over medium-high heat until lightly browned, about 1 minute.
Add hearts of palm mixture evenly to each toasted roll to serve.

vegan-enchiladas-with-lentil-and-sweet-p.name: Vegan Enchiladas With Lentil And Sweet Potato
vegan-enchiladas-with-lentil-and-sweet-p.original_name: Enchiladas veganas con lenteja y camote

ingredients.item: 3 cups (tazas) de camote cortado en cubos
¼ of avocado cut into cubes
1 rama de cilantro cortada finamente  (para decorar)

instructions: In a large frying pan, add the oil and heat with medium heat, put the garlic, onion and camote.
Fairy till the suit starts to glow. you don't have to get them cooked a lot because they'll get purée, it's just to glow them and create crispy cover.
Agrega las lentejas, la sal, mezcla y prueba los sabores.
It purifies the oven at 350ºF.
En un molde para horno pincela con un poco de aceite en la parte de abajo, cubre bien el fondo.
Warm up tortillas so they are soft and put two or three tablespoons of pilling, roll up and put in the oven mold with double down. Repeat with all tortillas.
Mete al horno por 20 minutos.Termina las enchiladas con rebanadas de aguacate, cilantro.

vegetable-stock.name: Vegetable Stock
instructions: Heat oil in a large stockpot over medium-high heat. Add remaining ingredients and cook, stirring occasionally, until vegetables begin to soften, 5-7 minutes. Add 4 quarts cold water. Bring to a boil; reduce heat and simmer until stock is reduced by half, 1-1 1/2 hours.
Strain stock through a fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl; discard solids. DO AHEAD: Stock can be made 3 days ahead. Let cool completely, then cover and chill, or freeze for up to 3 months.

vegetarian-butternut-squash-coconut-curr.name: Vegetarian Butternut Squash Coconut Curry Soup
instructions: Preheat the oven to 218°C (220 degrees C).

Cut ends off of butternut squash and peel with a vegetable peeler. Cut in half lengthwise, scoop out seeds and strings, and cut into 3/4-inch cubes. Put butternut squash into a 9x13-inch roasting pan and drizzle with olive oil; toss to coat. Mix in coconut, garam masala, curry powder, salt, and pepper; toss to cover squash evenly with the spices.
Roast in the preheated oven until butternut squash pieces pierce easily with a fork, 25 to 35 minutes.

Transfer roasted squash in batches to a food processor and add enough broth to cover squash. Process on low setting for 15 to 20 seconds. Repeat with remaining roasted squash. Transfer to a pot and heat until warm.

venezuelan-leaf-cachapa-recipe-comedera.name: Venezuelan Leaf Cachapa Recipe
venezuelan-leaf-cachapa-recipe-comedera.original_name: Receta de cachapa de hoja venezolana
instructions: Empieza pelando los jojotos hasta retirar todas las hebras.
Uncover bad leaves and reserve useful ones.
Desgrana los jojotos con un cuchillo filoso y tritura en un molino o procesador de alimentos.
In a bowl, mix ground jujotos with salt, sugar and ½ cup of water.
It integrates the ingredients until a homogenous mixture remains.
Toma las hojas de jojoto y dejar un hueco en la parte superior.
Fold half up and fill with dough using a small tablespoon and then place another leaf to the opposite.
Fold the leaf cages by half with a strap made from the same jujoto leaves, about 1 cm wide. You can also tie them with a stick.
Once the mount has been completed, place 5 cups of water with 2 tablespoons of salt at the bottom of the pot and place a layer of leaves to hold them while cooking and let them boil.
Enter the cages into the boiling pot, one by one and at a vertical position.
The approximate cooking time is 45 minutes.
To figure out if the cages are ready, hit with their fingers, they have to sound hollow (as a drum).
Baja inmediatamente la temperatura y retira las cachapas del agua.
Deja sobre un colador por 10 minutos y luego disfruta.

veracruz-style-red-snapper.name: Veracruz-style Red Snapper
instructions: Preheat the oven to 218°C (220 degrees C).
Make the sauce: Heat oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add onion; cook and stir until translucent, 6 to 7 minutes. Stir in garlic and cook until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Stir in capers and caper juice. Add tomatoes, olives, and jalapeño; cook and stir until tomatoes begin to collapse and jalapeño softens, about 3 minutes.
Remove from the heat and stir in oregano.
Make the snapper: Brush 1 teaspoon oil over the bottom of two small baking dishes. In each dish, add 1 tablespoon sauce, followed by a snapper fillet.
Season with cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper. Cover with more sauce, then drizzle lime juice over top.
Bake in the preheated oven until fish flakes easily with a fork, 15 to 20 minutes.

very-moist-and-flavorful-roast-turkey.name: Very Moist And Flavorful Roast Turkey

ingredients.prep: peeled, and cut into 8 wedges each

instructions: Cut butter into 1-tablespoon pieces, quarter the pieces, and refrigerate butter until needed.

Use your fingers to loosen the skin over the turkey breast and thighs. Cut a hole in the turkey skin between tail and body (to affix legs); cut a hole on each side of the turkey beneath each wing (to affix wings). Rub the entire skin with vegetable oil.
Toss apple and onion wedges, garlic cloves, and celery in a large bowl until combined; sprinkle poultry seasoning over the apple mixture and toss again to coat.
Stuff the body and neck cavities with the apple mixture. Insert each wing tip into the hole under the wing to secure wings; insert both legs into hole near tail to secure legs.
Place turkey onto a rack and set in a roasting pan. Insert cold butter pieces under the loosened skin, distributing the butter as evenly as possible.

Roast the turkey in the preheated oven until an instant-read meat thermometer inserted into the thickest part of a thigh, not touching bone, reads 71°C (70 degrees C), about 3 1/2 hours. Check for doneness after 3 hours.
Remove roasting pan containing turkey from oven and cover with aluminum foil, lightly pressing the foil directly onto the bird. Let the turkey rest for 40 minutes before carving.

viet-chile-sauce.name: Viet Chile Sauce

ingredients.prep:
jalapeños, or other medium-hot chiles, cut
into  0.5" pieces

ingredients.item: . (or more) agave nectar or mild honey
Diamond crystal or  0.75 tsp. morton kosher salt

instructions: Bring 2 garlic cloves, coarsely chopped, 4 oz. Fresno chiles, jalapeños, or other medium-hot chiles, cut into ½" pieces, 3 Tbsp. tomato paste, 2 Tbsp. agave nectar or mild honey, 2 Tbsp. distilled white vinegar, 1½ tsp. Diamond Crystal or ¾ tsp. Morton kosher salt, and ¾ cup water to a simmer in a small saucepan over medium heat with the exhaust fan on (to deal with the volatile chile fumes that will soon be floating in the air). Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer until chiles are softened and flavors have come together, about 4 minutes. Taste; if it’s too hot, add more agave nectar 1½ tsp. at a time. (The amount will depend on the spiciness of the chiles and your heat preference; the finished sauce will taste sweeter than it does during cooking, so don’t overdo it.) Continue to cook 4 minutes more. Remove from heat; let sit 5 minutes to allow flavors to concentrate.

Transfer sauce to a blender and purée until very smooth. Sprinkle ¼ tsp. xanthan gum over if using (it will thicken the sauce slightly) and blend just to combine. Let sauce sit, uncovered, 10 minutes.

Taste sauce; add more agave nectar, vinegar, salt, and/or some water (to dilute) if needed. Transfer to a small jar or airtight container; let cool before covering.
Do ahead: Sauce can be made 6 months ahead. Cover and chill.

vinegar-marinated-chicken-with-buttered-.name: Vinegar-marinated Chicken With Buttered Greens And Radishes

ingredients.item: Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper

instructions: Season chicken with salt and pepper and place in a large baking dish. Pour ¼ cup vinegar over chicken and let sit 15–20 minutes. Remove chicken from marinade and pat skin dry. Reserve baking dish (no need to wipe it out).

Preheat oven to 400°. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium. Working in batches, cook chicken, skin side down, until skin is golden brown and crisp, 8–10 minutes; turn and cook until other side is just browned, about 4 minutes. Transfer chicken to reserved baking dish; reserve skillet. Bake chicken until cooked through and an instant-read thermometer inserted into thickest part registers 165°, 10–12 minutes.

Meanwhile, heat butter in same skillet over medium-high. Add radishes, season with salt and pepper, and cook, stirring occasionally, until radishes are browned and tender, about 5 minutes. Add mustard greens and toss to coat; season with salt and pepper. Cook, stirring occasionally, until mustard greens are just wilted, about 2 minutes (they should still have some spring in their step). Add 2 Tbsp. tarragon and remaining 1 Tbsp. vinegar; toss to combine.
Serve greens and radishes with chicken topped with remaining 2 Tbsp. tarragon.
